Dashboard Archive: 2026-05-06 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: Momentum-Ranker Today Today's data shows two exceptional performers at the top of the momentum rankings, with DOCN (#1, score 97.3) and WDC (#2) displaying textbook strong momentum characteristics across multiple timeframes. DOCN's one-year return of 391% is paired with consistent ratio scaling (ratios of 1.3x to 2.16x between timeframes) and passes all four SMA gates, though its momentum signal of 4.0 is flagged as "overextended"—meaning gains may be stretched relative to recent volatility. WDC shows even more extreme long-term performance with a 973% one-year return, but that extreme ratio of 12.26x from 3-month to 1-year returns indicates the bulk of gains came earlier rather than recent momentum. Both stocks maintain above-average resilience versus SPY downdays (bad_spy_scores of 2.38 and 1.34 respectively, lower is better), suggesting they don't collapse in market selloffs. The contrast between these two leaders suggests the ranking rewards both consistent near-term momentum (DOCN style) and longer-cycle strength with solid recent support (WDC style).
For educational and informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
